feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: explain why i need a math coprocessor (fpu whatever) in my pc if i cant do math …
entry.content: explain why i need a math coprocessor (fpu whatever) in my pc if i cant do math beyond a 3rd grade level and use my pc for microsoft paint + nature videos i cant find one newer than like 1992 without this punkass tech

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Is there an honest reason /v/ hates competitive games so much? I get why certain…
entry.content: Is there an honest reason /v/ hates competitive games so much? I get why certain games are shit, but 1v1 skill based games like street fighter or age of empires 2, that reward you purely for how good you are at the game; your sheer practice, tactics, and willpower and have plenty of online opponents to fight of varying skill levels... how is this any different from a singleplayer game?? Why does v seethe so much at the idea??

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Hi /tv/, I'm writing a comedy with a mix of surreal humor from *Father Ted*, *Fa…
entry.content: Hi /tv/, I'm writing a comedy with a mix of surreal humor from *Father Ted*, *Family Guy*, and *The Simpsons*. I have tons of gags, but I can't come up with any storylines. It's about three friends. Can you help me?

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: I truly hate that movies have come to this point. It's just all so...samey. Wher…
entry.content: I truly hate that movies have come to this point. It's just all so...samey. Where's the Technicolor? Where's the bombastic expressive freedom? It's all just bland and paint-by-numbers. We honestly need a budget cap like baseball has salary caps, limitations are the mother of creativity and just throwing money at every problem clearly hasn't worked

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: First time playing RDR2
entry.content: >half the game so far spent saving, helping or working with niggers or native americans >not a single dark skinned NPC that's your enemy or evil at any point 20 hours in >all towns are extremely diverse in 1880s America >everywhere I go, there are women shouting they deserve voting rights >camp is full of girlboss characters who don't need no man The game's pretty good and the graphics and sound design are fucking amazing, but I had no idea how woke it'd be. I can only imagine what to expect wit

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: >Sonic Riders was considered "another bad Sonic game" >It was the only Sonic gam…
entry.content: >Sonic Riders was considered "another bad Sonic game" >It was the only Sonic game that gen that I geniunely enjoyed and still do >People finally began to understand how good it is and even has a competitive scene now Shame the idea is now dead thanks to Crossworlds but the resurgence of it has been cool, I think even the sequel is being seen more positively now.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Will you at least give it a chance?
entry.content: Will you at least give it a chance?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Why are "classic" survival horror games considered retro or outdated? I've been …
entry.content: Why are "classic" survival horror games considered retro or outdated? I've been playing them like pic related, flesh made fear, tormented souls, and currently RE: Code Veronica X, and I feel like they are not outdated. They feel more like video games than modern games. There are some things that are a chore, mostly too many loading screens, but other than that, they are fun.

entry.title: /b/ Random: Married vs Single
entry.content: Is it just from my experience or is it a common thing amongst these women? Whenever I've been brave enough to ask a woman who's married if she would be interested in doing anything sexual with another man, most likely she'll either: consider it or say yes but rarely say no. Then there's the single women who are more likely to decline immediately. Are married women more adventurous and open than single women? If so, what do you think the reason is?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Do you have CS degree?
entry.content: Anyone here who wasted their childhood or youth studying programming? There must be plenty of nerds on 4chan who used to grind Codeforces, Leetcode, Codewars, and similar garbage. How does it feel to realize you wasted your time for nothing? Nowadays, any random guy with a Claude Code AI agent can program without even knowing what variables and loops are. But the biggest losers are those who wasted their childhood and youth at universities studying Computer Science. You are obsolete now. Your in

Whether it's just doodles, smut art, or full-blown anthro stories/greentexts, all are welcome to share and critique. Let the Caturday festivities begin! Drop your favorite cat pics, comics, and game recs below! >Alpha Lexa Greens: https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/ folders/1Og2_VSZpZntiOqNK85_ANkFME- f77LsF?sort=13&direct

entry.title: /news/ News: Cyclospora 'Explosive Diarrhea’ Cases Reported in at Least 31 States
entry.content: A parasitic infection that causes miserable bouts of “explosive” diarrhea is surging across the United States at rates that appear to be higher than this time last year. Meanwhile, public health authorities are racing to identify the source or sources driving the surge. As of July 10, over 3,000 cases of cyclosporiasis have been reported, according to NBC News’ latest tally based on information from state health departments. Meanwhile, the U.S. Centers of Disease Control and Prevention confirmed

entry.title: /mu/ Music: My three least favourite trends in music, no particular order: 1 - Heavy use of …
entry.content: My three least favourite trends in music, no particular order: 1 - Heavy use of gated reverb on drums, 80s style 2 - The excessive vocal processing/vocoder use in 10s-20s pop 3 - Addition of fake vinyl noise, especially prevalent in millennial electronic music what are yours?

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /slop/ - AI Slop and Image Generation Thread #2988
entry.content: >Fresh Sushi Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. Previous Thread: https://desuarchive.org/trash/threa d/84012515 >Scripts to check image metadata: rentry.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: g5 quad water cooling issues
entry.content: to my knowledge, the g5 quad (with two dual-core processors) is the only macintosh that ever came with water cooling from the factory. and, they're notoriously unreliable for leaking fluids/ruining components nowadays. was that just how things were in ~2005? im sure the tech was in its infancy still, but idk if it was just a particularly bad example from its era.

Keep the thread alive Provide a image/video reference or link(s) Be patient, don't complain, and check the previous thread for requests that have already been made/fulfilled SFW thread: >>>/wsr/1555529 Previous Thread: https://desua

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Leetcode in 2026
entry.content: What's the point of Leetcode in 2026? We don't code anymore. Does your company still have leetcode interview rounds? I understand that leetcode was nothing more than a candidate filter. But back then, they at least tried to explain it by saying we were checking "how a candidate can handle corner cases," "how a candidate can write code and think," and so on. Now, even such weak arguments can't be made, because all of that is simply a thing of the past. We might as well ask candidates to juggle th

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: God I fucking hate Edinburgh. It's absolutely full of foreign fucks and English …
entry.content: God I fucking hate Edinburgh. It's absolutely full of foreign fucks and English cunts. Posh English twats have moved here in droves and bought up all the property, treating it like some romantic getaway. We've also got more jeets and shit skins than I can stand. The whole city went from trainspotting to Notting Hill overnight. Everyone's a tourist and thinks it's Harry Potter land. Fuck.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: The reason 1979's Born Again took three years to sink in for me was that Newman …
entry.content: The reason 1979's Born Again took three years to sink in for me was that Newman never pinned down the distance between himself and the creeps he wrote his first-person songs about. Because he's gained control as a singer, his oafish drawl here turns into a unifying voice, and the accompaniments are as eloquently integral as the American-colloquial pastiche of his Ragtime soundtrack. So this time the baffled racist of "Christmas in Capetown" and the happy-go-lucky Disney hero of "I'm Different" a

entry.title: /mu/ Music: ITT: post hardcore/ emotional hardcore/ noise rock and similar genres. What have…
entry.content: ITT: post hardcore/ emotional hardcore/ noise rock and similar genres. What have you been listening to lately? Favourite bands? Recs? Currently really enjoying Truck Violence's two albums, pretty nice stuff. Saw them a few weeks ago as supporting act for Show me the body. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IBo tnvWeXNs&list=RDfwh-SKn1HrE Favourite bands are probably Don Martin Three, Native Nod, Chino Horde, Moss Icon

entry.title: /mu/ Music: For about 15 years now, I've operated under a schizo theory: that AI will make m…
entry.content: For about 15 years now, I've operated under a schizo theory: that AI will make music that is not just more popular than human-made music but which is (in many cases) objectively and omnisubjectively far better than the best music made by the greatest human musicians in history. Such music may not be "objectively" "better" across literally every single metric; knowing something was made by a human will impart more emotional value, and some listeners may forever highly prioritize that. Just, as pu

entry.title: /o/ Auto: I haven't posted here in a long time, but here's my car atm. It's a 2012 370Z. S…
entry.content: I haven't posted here in a long time, but here's my car atm. It's a 2012 370Z. So far I've got: -Z1 csc bypass kit -Z1 lightweight flywheel and race clutch -Vortech V3 supercharger -Z1 ported upper and lower intake manifold -Deastchwerks 1400cc injectors -Z1 return fuel system with billet rails -Walbro 525lph fuel pump -ac delete pulley -Z1 upgraded coil packs -Berk test pipes -ARK Grip dual exhaust (came with the car) -Setrab 34 row oil cooler -Koyorad radiator -AST 6 point rollcage -momo monte

entry.title: /int/ International: It's genuinely insane how selfish immigrants are. You are literally dooming your…
entry.content: It's genuinely insane how selfish immigrants are. You are literally dooming your brown/yellow child into a life of social exclusion, where the native light colored Aryans will make fun of them for looking like poop, where they'll get bullied at school, where they'll be much less romantically successful with women as adults. My people will be associated with doordashing, massage parlors, fruit picking, welfare leeches. And all for what? So my parents could live in a bigger house and drive an SUV?

entry.title: /mu/ Music: I'm learning Chinese and was wondering if you could recommend some Chinese langu…
entry.content: I'm learning Chinese and was wondering if you could recommend some Chinese language artists to try? At the moment I am listening to 王菲 but it's quite entry-level. From experiance I've found that I can get the overall speaking and cadence down very quickly by listening to songs in the language. It obviously also helps with picking out colloquial terms and providing some exposure to the culture that is different to picking up a simple-language book or newspaper. Obviously with Chinese that's very

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Y'know, when the dotcom bubble popped, the market fully recovered after like 15 …
entry.content: Y'know, when the dotcom bubble popped, the market fully recovered after like 15 years, but in that time, the internet basically became fully integrated into our daily lives. Even if the AI bubble pops this year, it's not going anywhere. The question is what will AI look like a decade from now?
